For bulk orders, prepare a standard hotel/restaurant furniture package checklist BEFORE doing the final count. When you identify 18 beds, immediately ask 'Do you also need mattresses, pillow sets, or bulk assembly labor packages?' This will increase AOV and show consultative selling, not just order-taking.

Upsell 1: Customer buying 18 beds for hotel but mattresses never mentioned. Upsell 2: Agent never suggested bulk assembly labor package (7 wardrobes + 11 TV units are complex installations). Upsell 3: Decor items or soft furnishings for hotel lobby/common areas never broached. These three missed opportunities likely cost ₹2-5L in additional revenue.

Lead with empathy before action: Before checking any system, immediately acknowledge the customer's frustration with a genuine apology — e.g., 'I'm truly sorry for the experience you've had, this is absolutely not the standard we hold ourselves to.'

Address the cancellation threat directly: When a customer mentions cancellation, do not ignore it. Respond with urgency — 'I completely understand why you'd feel that way, and I want to make sure we resolve this for you right now before you make that decision.'

Set clear expectations during a hold or check: Always tell the customer exactly what you are doing and how long it will take — 'I'm going to pull up your order right now, it should take about 60 seconds, and I will give you a full update.'

Acknowledge systemic failures explicitly: When a customer reports that multiple agents failed them, own it on behalf of the company — 'You should never have had to call multiple times without a clear answer, and I take responsibility for making this right today.'
